verdict_reasoning: Fibronectin is a secreted glycoprotein that binds transiently to cell-surface integrin receptors (α5β1, αVβ3) via its RGD and other adhesion domains. However, it lacks any transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or post-translational covalent tether. The protein is in reversible, wash-sensitive equilibrium with the soluble plasma and extracellular pools. Soluble plasma fibronectin circulates freely; cellular fibronectin is assembled into insoluble ECM fibrils (not anchored to the PM). No documented cell-state-induced, tissue-restricted, lysosomal, or dual-localization surface pool. The protein is recruited to the pericellular environment by integrin binding but does not maintain a stable non-covalent or covalent PM anchor.
